---
title: "05 Pooled Logistic regression"
author: "Yuze Zheng"
date: "2026-04-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
library(fastglm)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
load('dt+res_trad+dt_IPW.RData')
keep <- c('dt','res_trad','dt_IPW')
rm(list = setdiff(ls(), keep))
```

# ── build logistic regression  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
```{r}
# Create the design (model) matrix for regression
# Includes:
# - trt: treatment indicator
# - cal_time: time variable
# - cal_timesqr: squared time (to model non-linearity)
# - Interaction terms between treatment and time variables
mat_boot <- model.matrix(
  ~ trt + cal_time + cal_timesqr + trt:cal_time + trt:cal_timesqr ,
  dt_IPW
)

# Define the outcome variable 
Y <- dt_IPW$outcome
# Extract weights (likely inverse probability weights)
W <- dt_IPW$wt

# Fit a weighted logistic regression model using fastglm
# - x: design matrix
# - y: binary outcome
# - weights: observation weights
# - family: binomial with logit link (i.e., logistic regression)
model <- fastglm(x = mat_boot,
                   y = Y,
                   weights = W,
                   family = binomial(link = "logit"))
  
```

# ── risk estimation  ──────────────────────────────────────────────
```{r}
# -------------------------------
# Step 1: Create counterfactual datasets
# -------------------------------

# Create a new dataset representing the "no treatment" strategy
# for each time point
# Dataset assuming no treatment (trt = 0) across all time points
time_points = 0:59
notrt_data <- data.table(
    trt = 0L,
    cal_time = time_points,
    cal_timesqr = time_points^2
  )
# Dataset assuming treatment (trt = 1) across all time points 
trt_data <- data.table(
    trt = 1L,
    cal_time = time_points,
    cal_timesqr = time_points^2
  )
```

# -------------------------------
# Step 2: Construct model matrices
# -------------------------------
```{r}
# Build design matrices consistent with the fitted regression model
# Includes main effects and interaction terms (time-varying treatment effect)
mat_notrt <- model.matrix(~ trt + cal_time + cal_timesqr + trt:cal_time + trt:cal_timesqr,
                            notrt_data)
mat_trt <- model.matrix(~ trt + cal_time + cal_timesqr + trt:cal_time + trt:cal_timesqr,
                          trt_data)
# -------------------------------
# Step 3: Predict outcome probabilities
# -------------------------------
# Predict discrete-time risk (probability of outcome) at each time point
# under each treatment strategy using the fitted model
notrt_data$risk <- predict(model, mat_notrt, type = "response")
trt_data$risk <- predict(model, mat_trt, type = "response")
```

```{r}
# For the no-treatment group and treatment group
  # 1. calculate the average predicted risk at each time point
  # 2. accumulate those risks over time to get cumulative incidence
  # 3. multiply by 100 to express as a percentage
notrt_risk <- notrt_data[, .(risk_notrt = mean(risk)), by = cal_time
][, cuminc_notrt := cumsum(risk_notrt) * 100]
  
trt_risk <- trt_data[, .(risk_trt = mean(risk)), by = cal_time
][, cuminc_trt := cumsum(risk_trt) * 100]
  
```

```{r}
# -------------------------------
# Step 5: Compare treatment strategies
# -------------------------------

# Merge results by time
risk_results <- merge(trt_risk, notrt_risk, by = "cal_time")
  risk_results[, `:=`(
    risk_ratio = cuminc_trt / cuminc_notrt,
    risk_diff = cuminc_trt - cuminc_notrt
  )
  ][, c("risk_trt", "risk_notrt") := NULL]
  
```
